conditions.curing_method: cast and isothermally set for 24 h at initial temperature; demoulded, wrapped in cling film and returned to 20 ± 2 °C chamber
conditions.specimen_geometry: 40 x 40 x 160 mm bars; 40 mm cubes for compression

conditions.test_method: compressive strength measured using hydraulic press
conditions.specimen_geometry: 40 mm cubes cut from flexural bars

figures.caption: Table/figure page containing composition and curing conditions.
Table 3. Compressive strength and standard deviation of aerated concretes.

evidence.evidence_text: Study investigates composition, curing temperature and density on strength of aerated concretes based on Mk-FA geopolymeric binders using BFS as lightweight aggregate.
Composition and curing conditions list mixture names, Mk wt.%, FA wt.%, initial curing temperature, bulk density and aggregate ratio 1:1 with BFS for B mixes.
Concretes were cast and isothermally set at 20 ± 2 °C or 75 ± 5 °C for 24 h in acrylic moulds of 40 x 40 x 160 mm3; after demoulding wrapped and returned to 20 ± 2 °C.
Four cubes of 40 mm were cut from the tested bars and used for compressive strength measurements.
Table 3 reports compressive strength (MPa) and standard deviation for curing days 1, 4, 7, 14, 28, 90 and 180 for aerated concretes.
